title: Genista glabrescens
intro: Genista glabrescens, also known as the spineless broom or small broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Genista glabrescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Genista glabrescens is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to promote digestion and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Genista glabrescens is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Europe and has a classification within the subgenus Spartium. Genista glabrescens is further classified as a part of the genus Genista, which consists of broom plants. The plant's classification is also supported by its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system. This system is used to identify and categorize plants based on their characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
